original_prompt:
/autopilot The cross-competitor review analysis
  card ("Common Competitor Weaknesses") is not
  showing in the competitor intel section even though
   4 of 5 competitors have negative reviews.

  The module exists at research-worker/src/competitor
  s/review-cross-analysis.ts and is called in
  index.ts:154 via Promise.all with .catch(() =>
  null). The card component exists at src/components/
  workspace/cards/review-cross-analysis-card.tsx. The
   card taxonomy at
  src/lib/workspace/card-taxonomy.ts:342-362 creates
  the card when reviewCrossAnalysis data exists.

  Debug steps:
  1. Check
  research-worker/src/competitors/synthesize.ts — is
  the reviewCrossAnalysis data being injected into
  the final output? Search for where crossAnalysis is
   passed to postProcessSynthesis. Verify the data
  flows from index.ts through to the final JSON.
  2. Check if the postProcessSynthesis function at
  synthesize.ts actually writes reviewCrossAnalysis
  to the parsed object. It might be missing the
  injection step.
  3. Add console.log in index.ts after the
  Promise.all at line 154 to log whether
  crossAnalysis is null or has data.
  4. Check the card-taxonomy.ts filter at line 356 —    it requires affectedCompetitors.length >= 2. Verify
   the generated data meets this threshold.
  5. If the data IS being generated but not reaching
  the frontend, check the Supabase write — is           reviewCrossAnalysis included in the section data      that gets stored?
  The fix should ensure the cross-competitor review
  data flows: analyzeReviewCrossPatterns() → index.ts
   → postProcessSynthesis() → final JSON → Supabase →
   frontend card-taxonomy → card component.
  Build must pass after changes.
